name.text: Finding the volume of a given composite solid

questiontext.text:
A water tank consists of a cylindrical part with a hemispherical top. The radius of the tank is {@r@} m and the height of the cylindrical part is {@h_cyl@} m.

[[jsxgraph width="400px" height="400px"]] var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, { /* [left, top, right, bottom] - ensuring enough head-room for the dome */ boundingbox: [-10, 20, 10, -5], axis: false, showCopyright: false }); var R = {#r#}; var H = {#h_cyl#}; var p = 0.4; /* Perspective squash factor */ /* 1. The Hemispherical Dome (The Outer Arc) */ /* This MUST be drawn without the 'p' factor to look like a real dome */ board.create('curve', [ function(t){ return R * Math.cos(t); }, function(t){ return H + R * Math.sin(t); }, 0, Math.PI ], {strokeColor: 'black', strokeWidth: 3}); /* 2. The Seam (Where cylinder meets hemisphere) */ /* We only draw the FRONT half of this ellipse to show the 'edge' */ board.create('curve', [ function(t){ return R * Math.cos(t); }, function(t){ return H + (R * p) * Math.sin(t); }, Math.PI, 2 * Math.PI ], {strokeColor: 'black', strokeWidth: 2}); /* 3. Vertical Sides of the Cylinder */ board.create('segment', [[-R, 0], [-R, H]], {strokeColor: 'black', strokeWidth: 2}); board.create('segment', [[R, 0], [R, H]], {strokeColor: 'black', strokeWidth: 2}); /* 4. Base of Cylinder */ /* Front solid curve */ board.create('curve', [ function(t){ return R * Math.cos(t); }, function(t){ return (R * p) * Math.sin(t); }, Math.PI, 2 * Math.PI ], {strokeColor: 'black', strokeWidth: 2}); /* Back dashed curve */ board.create('curve', [ function(t){ return R * Math.cos(t); }, function(t){ return (R * p) * Math.sin(t); }, 0, Math.PI ], {strokeColor: 'black', strokeWidth: 1, dash: 2}); /* 5. Labels - Matching your color requirements */ // Height (Red) board.create('segment', [[-R - 2, 0], [-R - 2, H]], {strokeColor: 'red', strokeWidth: 2}); board.create('text', [-R - 5, H/2, "{#h_cyl#} m"], {color: 'red', fontSize: 16, fontWeight: 'bold'}); // Radius (Blue) board.create('segment', [[0, 0], [R, 0]], {strokeColor: 'blue', strokeWidth: 3}); board.create('text', [R/2, -1.5, "{#r#} m"], {color: 'blue', fontSize: 16, fontWeight: 'bold'}); [[/jsxgraph]]

Find the total volume of water the tank can hold. (Write \(\pi\) as %pi)

Volume = [[input:ans1]] \(m^3\) [[validation:ans1]] [[feedback:prt1]]

generalfeedback.text:
Worked solution

To find the total volume, we sum the cylindrical and hemispherical volumes.

\(\begin{align} \text{Volume of Cylinder } (V_1) &= \pi r^2 h \\ &= \pi \times {@r@}^2 \times {@h_cyl@} \\ &= {@V_cyl@} \text{ m}^3 \\\\ \text{Volume of Hemisphere } (V_2) &= \frac{2}{3} \pi r^3 \\ &= \frac{2}{3} \times \pi \times {@r@}^3 \\ &= {@V_hemi@} \text{ m}^3 \\\\ \text{Total Volume } (V) &= V_1 + V_2 \\ &= {@V_cyl@} + {@V_hemi@} \\ &={@ta@} \text{ m}^3 \end{align}\)

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.
